Pandowdy Apple IIe Emulator
Table of Contents

Class SystemStatusSnapshot

Namespace
Pandowdy.EmuCore.Services
Assembly
Pandowdy.EmuCore.dll

Immutable snapshot of all Apple IIe system status flags and soft switch states.

public record SystemStatusSnapshot : IEquatable<SystemStatusSnapshot>
Inheritance
object
SystemStatusSnapshot
Implements
IEquatable<SystemStatusSnapshot>
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object)
object.Equals(object, object)
object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.MemberwiseClone()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Remarks

Immutability: This record is immutable - once created, its state cannot change. Any modification requires creating a new snapshot via SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder. This ensures thread-safe reads and clear change tracking through the observable pattern.

Complete State Capture: Contains all 24 soft switch states for a single point in time, including memory configuration, video modes, and I/O settings. This allows consumers (renderers, debuggers, UI) to work with a consistent view of system state.

Apple IIe Context: These flags directly correspond to Apple IIe hardware soft switches and annunciators, providing authentic emulation of the system's state management. See Apple IIe Technical Reference Manual for detailed switch descriptions.

Constructors

SystemStatusSnapshot(bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool, bool)

Immutable snapshot of all Apple IIe system status flags and soft switch states.

public SystemStatusSnapshot(bool State80Store, bool StateRamRd, bool StateRamWrt, bool StateIntCxRom, bool StateAltZp, bool StateSlotC3Rom, bool StatePb0, bool StatePb1, bool StatePb2, bool StateAnn0, bool StateAnn1, bool StateAnn2, bool StateAnn3_DGR, bool StatePage2, bool StateHiRes, bool StateMixed, bool StateTextMode, bool StateShow80Col, bool StateAltCharSet, bool StateFlashOn, bool StatePrewrite, bool StateUseBank1, bool StateHighRead, bool StateHighWrite)

Parameters

State80Store bool

80STORE switch - redirects page 2 to aux memory ($C000/$C001).

StateRamRd bool

RAMRD switch - reads from aux memory ($C002/$C003).

StateRamWrt bool

RAMWRT switch - writes to aux memory ($C004/$C005).

StateIntCxRom bool

INTCXROM switch - uses internal ROM vs slot ROMs ($C006/$C007).

StateAltZp bool

ALTZP switch - uses aux zero page and stack ($C008/$C009).

StateSlotC3Rom bool

SLOTC3ROM switch - enables slot 3 ROM ($C00A/$C00B).

StatePb0 bool

Paddle button 0 state.

StatePb1 bool

Paddle button 1 state.

StatePb2 bool

Paddle button 2 state.

StateAnn0 bool

Annunciator 0 state ($C058/$C059).

StateAnn1 bool

Annunciator 1 state ($C05A/$C05B).

StateAnn2 bool

Annunciator 2 state ($C05C/$C05D).

StateAnn3_DGR bool

Annunciator 3 / Double Graphics state ($C05E/$C05F).

StatePage2 bool

PAGE2 switch - selects page 1 or 2 ($C054/$C055).

StateHiRes bool

HIRES switch - graphics vs text ($C056/$C057).

StateMixed bool

MIXED switch - mixed graphics and text ($C052/$C053).

StateTextMode bool

TEXT switch - text mode on/off ($C050/$C051).

StateShow80Col bool

80COL switch - 80-column display ($C00C/$C00D).

StateAltCharSet bool

ALTCHARSET switch - alternate character set ($C00E/$C00F).

StateFlashOn bool

Flash state for inverse/flashing characters (timing-based).

StatePrewrite bool

PREWRITE flag for language card bank switching.

StateUseBank1 bool

Language card bank selection (bank 1 vs bank 2).

StateHighRead bool

Language card read enable ($D000-$FFFF).

StateHighWrite bool

Language card write enable ($D000-$FFFF).

Remarks

Immutability: This record is immutable - once created, its state cannot change. Any modification requires creating a new snapshot via SystemStatusSnapshotBuilder. This ensures thread-safe reads and clear change tracking through the observable pattern.

Complete State Capture: Contains all 24 soft switch states for a single point in time, including memory configuration, video modes, and I/O settings. This allows consumers (renderers, debuggers, UI) to work with a consistent view of system state.

Apple IIe Context: These flags directly correspond to Apple IIe hardware soft switches and annunciators, providing authentic emulation of the system's state management. See Apple IIe Technical Reference Manual for detailed switch descriptions.

State80Store

80STORE switch - redirects page 2 to aux memory ($C000/$C001).
